publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-09-22 00:01:33 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, articles, clippings, reports, notebooks, and articles.
did.abstract: Mostly department correspondence, largely that of Gussie Esther Gaskill, first curator of the Wason Collection (1927-1963); materials concerning trips to China and Japan; projects; bibliographies; acquisitions, cataloging, and other records; accessions list; accounting statistics, and general office files, 1976-1985; annual reports of the East Asian Librarian, 1972-1988. Correspondence relating to Charles Wason's bequest, especially correspondence between Mrs. Charles Wason and the Arthur H. Clark Company, correspondence between Mrs. Wason and Gussie Gaskill, drawing and photographs of the library at the Wason home, and articles, reports and clippings about the collection. Three handwritten notebooks by T.F. Crane listing the Wason Collection, ca. 1922.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: The Wason Collection was bequeathed to Cornell University in 1918 by Charles William Wason. It began as a collection of English-language works on China and the Chinese. Its scope and size have since been greatly enlarged, and it is now the only major East Asian collection in the country to include all Western language materials in the areas covered in a special collection together with the materials in the languages of those areas.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University Wason Collection records, #13-6-1721. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Mostly department correspondence, largely that of Gussie Esther Gaskill, first curator of the Wason Collection (1927-1963); materials concerning trips to China and Japan; projects; bibliographies; acquisitions, cataloging, and other records; accessions list; accounting statistics, and general office files, 1976-1985; annual reports of the East Asian Librarian, 1972-1988. Correspondence relating to Charles Wason's bequest, especially correspondence between Mrs. Charles Wason and the Arthur H. Clark Company, correspondence between Mrs. Wason and Gussie Gaskill, drawing and photographs of the library at the Wason home, and articles, reports and clippings about the collection. Three handwritten notebooks by T.F. Crane listing the Wason Collection, ca. 1922.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Files from Thomas Hahn, Wason Curator until 2006, include materials about the Encyclopedia Maxima, Hu Shih, ships' logs, a portrait of Mao by Andy Warhol, other acquisitions, and exhibitions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous items relating to the Wason Collection include letters, description, and notes about the Ming Treasure Note, 1934; and letter about and translation of a Boxer Rebellion poster, 1921.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access to Boxes 2-4 restricted to the permission of the office of origin.

did.unittitle: Series I. Records related to Miss Gaskill's curatorship
General correspondence, filed alphabetically by the name of the correspondent
Miss Gaskill and the Far Eastern Bibliography,
Miss Gaskill and Far Eastern libraries
Miss Gaskill and the ALA-CCS Special Committee on Far Eastern Materials,
Miss Gaskill and Wason Collection Chinese book orders
Miss Gaskill and Japanese book orders
Series II. Wason material sent to Archives
Collaborative Studies of China and Japan Newsletter
Wason - Exhibit: "China and the West" (1966); "Asia and the West" ( 1974)
Department heads and designated assistants meeting notes ( 1978-1980)
National Repository for Chinese Agriculture Publications
U.S. corporations in China and Japan (1980)
Annual reports of the East Asian Librarian,
Wason miscellany including correspondence about Boxer Rebellion poster; correspondence, etc. about Ming currency; articles from Pacific Affairs (2 folders); "Translation of Euclid's Elements"
Correspondence with Mrs. Wason (mainly Gussie Gaskill)
Chinese dragon flag from Mrs. Wason
Three handwritten notebooks by T.F. Crane listing the Wason Collection, ca.
Cornell Rare and Manuscript Collections China related items and correspondence
Materials relating to the Yongle Encyclopedia Maxima

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes mateiar relating to the 600th anniversary symposium.

did.unittitle: Drawing of the Chinese library of Charles Wason at his residence.
